summary: Career Hub & Stoke Sponsor system testing complete. All 15 backend API tests passed (100%). All 3 frontend pages (The Peak, The Inside, Stoke Sponsor Dashboard) load and function correctly. Fixed critical bug: elite_tier column type mismatch - changed from Enum to String in model and updated all comparisons to use string values instead of enum.

career_stats_api.details: Returns career stats including elite_tier, world_ranking, career_points, and competition stats

competition_results_get.details: Returns list of competition results with event details and verification status

competition_results_post.details: Creates new competition results with event_name, event_date, placing, heat_wins, etc.

sponsorships_api.details: Returns sponsorships list with filtering by active_only parameter

stoke_sponsor_eligible.details: Returns eligible surfers with tier filtering (grom_rising, competitive, pro_elite)

stoke_sponsor_contributions.details: Returns total_contributed, contribution_count, and contributions list

the_peak_hub_page.details: Page loads with data-testid='the-peak-hub', shows Competition Stats, Recent Results, Sponsorship Manager

the_inside_hub_page.details: Page loads with data-testid='the-inside-hub', shows Road to The Peak progress, Grom Series Results

stoke_sponsor_dashboard_page.details: Page loads with data-testid='stoke-sponsor-dashboard', shows impact stats, surfer filters, and 50 eligible surfers

elite_tier_enum_error.issue: PostgreSQL error: type 'elitetierenum' does not exist
elite_tier_enum_error.root_cause: Model defined elite_tier as Enum(EliteTierEnum) but database column was VARCHAR
elite_tier_enum_error.fix: Changed elite_tier column from Enum to String(50) in models.py and updated all comparisons in career.py to use string literals instead of enum values

critical_code_review_comments: elite_tier column type inconsistency fixed - now using String type throughout
All Career Hub API endpoints properly mounted and responding
Stoke Sponsor system properly calculates platform fees (5% for groms, 10% for others)
Frontend pages have proper data-testid attributes for testing
Route protection working correctly - redirects to auth when not logged in
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_ prefixed competition results and sponsorships during backend testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Career Hub system fully functional. elite_tier is now VARCHAR in database, comparisons use string values ('pro_elite', 'competitive', 'grom_rising'). Stoke Sponsor dashboard shows 50 eligible surfers with tier filters. All data-testid attributes in place for frontend testing.
